column1: # Chroma precision A/B: libwebp-exact (×4) vs zenwebp-tuned (byte-round) chroma downsampling
# Question: should byte-exact chroma be the DEFAULT (not just parity)?
# Method: Y held constant (zenyuv default Y both variants); only source U/V precision varies.
#   Encode ZenwebpDefault method 4, decode, zensim (ZensimProfile::latest) vs original.
# Corpus: 3 CID22-512 + 12 imazen-26 (color-rich categories), imazen resized to <=1024 max-dim Lanczos3.
# CONCLUSION: WASH on real content. Overall mean dzsim(E-T)=-0.038, dsize=-0.05% (both noise).
#   Tuned keeps a small edge only at q20 (-0.195). No real-content case to switch the default;
#   keep tuned byte-round (also passes the synthetic zensim floor gate). Parity uses exact.
# Scope note: single size regime (<=1024) + method 4 only; A/B decision (keep), not a calibration constant.
# chroma precision sweep: EXACT(×4) vs TUNED(byte-round), Y held constant, method 4
OVERALL mean Δzsim(E−T) = -0.038  |  Δsize%(E−T) = -0.05  (positive Δzsim ⇒ EXACT better)
